lines.en_lit: does not accept writing As we learned Rabbi Yehuda says: There was only one pedestal What is the reason not two because exchanged But let us place two and write on them which is for the bull and which is for the goat. Rather, Rabbi Yehuda does not accept writing

raises an objection There were thirteen horns in the Temple, and they were inscribed New shekels. And old shekels Nests, young birds for burnt-offerings; wood, frankincense; and gold for the Ark cover. And six for free-will offerings

New shekels, these are shekels of each and every year old shekels, one who did not donate a shekel the previous year, donate the next year; nests, these are the turtledoves young birds for burnt-offerings, these are pigeons; and all these are burnt-offerings; the statement of Rabbi Yehuda.

When Rav Dimi came he said: They say in the West, decree due to a sin-offering whose owners have died. And are we concerned But didn’t we learn one who sends his sin-offering from overseas, they sacrifice it on the presumption that he is alive,
Rather, due to a sin-offering whose owner has certainly died, let us select four zuz, and throw them into the water and these will be permitted. Rabbi Yehuda does not accept clarification.

From where do we If we say from that which we learned one who buys wine from among the Samaritans, on Shabbat eve at nightfall arise and say: Two log which I will separate in the future, shall be teruma
